page: OVHcloud b3-256 is a general-purpose server instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated physical cores with one thread per core, utilizing AMD EPYC Milan processors running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The instance is configured with 256 GB of RAM, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is provided via a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 16 Gbps. The virtualization layer is managed by the KVM hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-threaded and memory performance. The multi-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests ranks in the top 10% of tested systems, aided by the dedicated, non-hyperthreaded core allocation. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks also places in the top 10%, while large block read and write operations rank in the top 25%. In application-specific tests, the server demonstrates top-tier capabilities in software compilation, ray tracing, image processing, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Redis SET operations and prompt processing for small language models also achieve top-tier throughput.

Given its dedicated hardware profile and high resource density, the b3-256 offers a cost-efficient solution for workloads requiring sustained CPU performance without the overhead of hyperthreading. The balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and fast NVMe storage make it highly suitable for multi-threaded applications, software compilation pipelines, database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: The OVHcloud b3-256 is a general-purpose server instance built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by AMD EPYC Milan processors. It features 64 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, operating at 2.3 GHz, paired with 256 GB of RAM and a 400 GB NVMe SSD. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, software compilation speeds, and memory bandwidth, alongside strong database and static web serving capabilities. This hardware profile provides a cost-efficient, high-density resource pool for demanding multi-threaded workloads. It is highly suited for software development pipelines, database hosting, static web serving, and moderate-scale language model inference.
og_description: OVHcloud b3-256 is a general-purpose KVM instance with 64 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 256 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for demanding workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud b3-256 is a general-purpose KVM instance featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 256 GB RAM, and 400 GB NVMe storage.
tagline: A dedicated sixty-four core AMD EPYC server with balanced memory and NVMe storage for high-throughput multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan physical cores with one thread per core
256 GB of system memory and 400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and one public IPv4 address
Top-tier multi-core CPU, software compilation, and memory bandwidth benchmarks
Well-suited for database operations, CI/CD pipelines, and web serving
